valencian-rice-with-chard-arros-amb-bled.name: Valencian Rice with Chard (arròs Amb Bledes)
valencian-rice-with-chard-arros-amb-bled.original_name: Arroz con acelgas valenciano (Arròs amb bledes)

ingredients.item: 200 g accelerates (only the most tender leaves and sizes)
1 tomate maduro o 60 g de tomate triturado
750 ml de caldo de verduras o de carne

instructions: Empezamos preparando todos los ingredientes. Lavamos bien las acelgas, retiramos las hebras gruesas de los tallos si hiciera falta, y cortamos tanto las hojas como los tallos tiernos en trozos medianos.
We peel the potato and cut it into small crapes and shove it with our hands to release the starch, with a similar size to cook them as well.
Pelamos y picamos media cebolla y un diente de ajo. Rallamos el tomate con un rallador fino. Reservamos.
Ponemos el caldo a calentar en un cazo aparte y lo mantenemos caliente.
In a widely-built pan, we warm olive oil with medium heat. We added onion and garlic and we pitted them up until they are clear.
We put in the sweet pimenton, quickly remove so that it doesn't get burned and add the pumped or crushed tomato. We cook this socorro for 5 minutes.
We added the chipboard and the potato. We mix everything well with the frying to integrate flavors.
Then we put up the rice and scattering with the other ingredients for a few minutes.

Let's check hot broth, saving some to adjust texture while cooking if necessary. Let's move the casserole a little to distribute the ingredients well. When you start boiling, we add baked and drained beans.

We rectilate salt, add some saffron strands and let the whole cook with medium heat for about 20 minutes, or the time indicated in the rice package. If we see it dries too much, we add more hot stock.
When the rice's at its point, we put the pan out of the fire and serve immediately to avoid passing.

vanilla-cashew-vegan-ice-cream.name: Vanilla Cashew Vegan Ice Cream

ingredients.item: Plus 2 tablespoons organic or granulated sugar

instructions: Place vanilla bean and cashews in a large bowl and add 2 cups boiling water. Cover and let sit at room temperature at least 12 hours (hydrating the cashews thoroughly is key for a silky, smooth ice cream).

Transfer cashews and their soaking liquid to a blender. Scrape in seeds from vanilla bean; discard pod. Add sugar, and salt. Purée until smooth, about 30 seconds. With the motor running on medium speed, slowly stream in oil and purée until thick and creamy.

Immediately process cashew mixture in ice cream maker according to manufacturer’s directions. (Head’s up: this will take longer than a traditional dairy-based custard to reach soft-serve consistency.) Do not chill before processing or oil will harden into tiny bits and texture will be grainy.
Transfer to an airtight container and press plastic wrap directly onto surface. Freeze until firm, at least 4 hours.
Do Ahead: Cashews can be soaked 3 days ahead; cover and chill. Ice cream can be made 1 month ahead; keep frozen.

vegan-strawberry-gelato.name: Vegan Strawberry Gelato
instructions: Mix lemon juice and cornstarch together in a small bowl and pour into a blender. Add strawberries, soy milk, sugar, sweetener, and salt; blend until smooth.

Pour mixture into a pot. Heat over medium heat, stirring frequently to keep from burning, until mixture comes to a boil and starts to thicken, 10 to 15 minutes. Cover and chill thoroughly, 8 hours to overnight.

Pour mixture into an ice cream maker and freeze according to manufacturer's instructions, in 2 batches if necessary, about 20 minutes. Transfer to an airtight container and freeze until firm, about 4 hours.

vegan-street-tacos.name: Vegan Street Tacos

ingredients.item: Diced white or red onion and chopped fresh cilantro for serving

instructions: Heat 1 tsp. vegetable oil in large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Add ½ onion, sliced and 2 tsp. chopped seeded jalapeño; cook, stirring, until tender, about 10 minutes. Add 1 12-ounce package soy chorizo, casing removed and cook until beginning to brown in spots, stirring often, about 5 minutes.
Meanwhile, cook 1 15.4–16-oz. can vegetarian refried black beans in heavy small saucepan over low heat until heated through, stirring occasionally.

Stack 2 warm corn tortillas for each of 6 tacos. Spread scant 2 Tbsp. beans over each stack. Top with soy chorizo mixture, dividing equally. Sprinkle with diced white or red onion and chopped fresh cilantro.


Editor’s note: This recipe was first printed in our September 2009 issue. Head this way for more of our best vegan recipes →

vegan-tacos-al-pastor.name: Vegan Tacos Al Pastor

ingredients.prep: halved and cut into thin slices

instructions: Place guajillo peppers in a saucepan, cover with water, and boil until softened, about 10 minutes. Drain.

Combine lime juice, 2 cloves garlic, cumin, and cloves in a small saucepan over medium heat; bring to a boil. Remove from heat and cool for 5 minutes. Pour into a blender, add guajillo peppers, and blend until smooth. Add 1 to 2 tablespoons of the water if the sauce is too thick.

Heat oil in a large saucepan over medium heat and cook onion slices until soft and translucent, about 5 minutes. Stir in vegetable protein; cook and stir for 2 minutes. Pour in guajillo sauce and remaining water; season with salt. Bring to a boil; reduce heat to medium-low cook, covered, until flavors combine, about 10 minutes.
Set oven rack about 6 inches from the heat source and preheat the oven's broiler. Combine tomatoes and chile de arbol peppers in a baking dish.
Roast tomatoes and peppers until charred, 5 to 10 minutes. Cool tomatoes slightly. Peel.
Combine peeled tomatoes, peppers, 1 slice onion, 1 clove garlic, and salt in a blender; blend until smooth. Pour salsa into a small bowl.
Warm corn tortillas in a skillet. Divide vegetable protein mixture amongst tortillas and top with salsa, chopped onion, and cilantro.

vegetable-pizza-recipe-comedera.name: Vegetable Pizza Recipe
instructions: Inicia la receta haciendo la masa. Para ello, mezcla en un bol la harina, la sal y el polvo de hornear.
Abre un espacio en el medio de la mezcla de los ingredientes secos y vierte el agua y el aceite. Con las manos, ve uniendo todo hasta que la masa se vaya formando.
It's good till you get a smooth texture. Make your dough a ball, put them in a bowl with a moist cloth and put them in a cool place.
Preheat the oven at 180 ° C
Take the broccoli and separate the balls, if they are too big, cut them half.
Take onion and peppers, pele them and cut them into thin strips. Reserve.
Ralla el queso mozzarella.
Toma una bandeja y engrasa.
Enharina tu encimera y estira la masa muy bien. Pasa a la bandeja y adapta su forma.
Esparce la salsa de tomate en la masa e incorpora el queso mozzarella.
Then put the broccoli, onion and peppers. It ends by adding the spoonful of well distributed oregano onto pizza.
Once your pizza's baked for about 20 to 25 minutes till the dough's well golden and the vegetables are cooked.
Remove pizza from the oven and cut into portions. Enjoy!

vegetarian-carbonara.name: Vegetarian Carbonara
instructions: Cook pasta in a large pot of boiling generously salted water, stirring occasionally and adding asparagus when pasta has 1 minute left to go, until pasta is very al dente, about 3 minutes less than package directions.

Meanwhile, combine garlic and oil in a medium Dutch oven or other high-sided pot and set over medium heat; season very generously with pepper. Cook, stirring often, until garlic is light golden, about 2 minutes. Remove from heat and stir in paprika; set aside.
Place egg yolks in a medium bowl. Scoop out 1 cup cooking liquid from pasta and, whisking constantly, gradually add to egg yolks to temper. Season with salt and pepper; set aside.

Drain pasta and asparagus, reserving 2 cups pasta cooking liquid; transfer pasta and asparagus to pot with reserved garlic oil. Set back over medium heat and toss to coat. Tossing constantly, add 1 cup pasta cooking liquid, followed by reserved yolk mixture, then ¾ cup Parmesan. Cook, tossing constantly and reducing heat as needed to keep mixture below a simmer, until cheese is melted and liquid thickens enough to coat pasta in a glossy, silky sauce, about 5 minutes. (This might be easier on your hands if you use 2 wooden spoons, as though you were tossing a salad, rather than trying to do it with 1 hand and a pair of tongs.) Add more reserved pasta cooking liquid if needed to loosen.
Divide pasta among shallow bowls and top with more Parmesan and a few grinds of pepper.

vegetarian-mexican-casserole.name: Vegetarian Mexican Casserole

ingredients.item: Uncle ben's® instant whole grain brown rice
Uncle ben's® ready rice® spanish style

instructions: Preheat oven to 191°C.

Heat oil in large skillet over medium-high heat. Add garlic and cook until aromatic, about 30 seconds. Add tomatillos and cook, stirring often, until tender, about 5 minutes. Stir in black beans, tomatoes, cumin, ancho powder, salt, and cook, smashing beans and tomatoes with a spoon, until warmed through, 5 minutes. Add brown and Spanish rice. Bring to boil, then reduce heat and simmer until rice is tender and warmed through, 2 minutes. Remove from heat.

Spread 1 1/2 cups of rice mixture into a 9x13-inch baking dish. Sprinkle with 1/2 cup chips. Repeat process with remaining rice mixture and chips. Top with cheese and bake in preheated oven until cheese is melted and bubbling, about 15 minutes.
Top with radishes and cilantro. Serve immediately.

vinaigrette-or-chinese-salad-sauce-peque.name: Vinaigrette Or Chinese Salad Sauce
vinaigrette-or-chinese-salad-sauce-peque.original_name: Vinagreta o Salsa para Ensalada China
instructions: In a small bowl, dissolve the sugar and salt into the rice vinegar. It's important to make sure that the sugar and salt dissolve completely to prevent granules from remaining in the vinegar.
Little by little while you're still beating, add sunflower oil. Bate constantly to emulsify the mixture, that's to say, so that oil and vinegar are evenly combined.

Test his vinegar and suit his taste. If you prefer a more sweet vinegar, you can add some more sugar. If you like more acid, add some more vinegar. If the sauce's too salty, you can dilute it by adding some more vinegar from rice or water. Another option is to add more sugar to balance the taste.
Esta vinagreta se puede usar de inmediato sobre ensaladas frescas o guardarla en la nevera por hasta una semana. Recuerda batirla de nuevo antes de usarla si los ingredientes se separan.

vinegar.name: Vinegar
instructions: To begin, understand that successful vinegar making relies on either a pre-existing "vinegar mother" (which can be ordered online) or a heavy dose of unfiltered raw vinegar (like Bragg’s Cider Vinegar). The raw vinegar contains the necessary acetic acid bacteria to trigger fermentation, which will likely grow a new visible mother and a pellicle (a thin cellulose layer).
In a nonreactive (preferably glass) container, combine the dry red (or white) wine and the raw apple cider vinegar.
Cover the opening of the container with cheesecloth or a clean kitchen towel and secure it with a rubber band. Place the container in a dark location.

Stir and taste the mixture occasionally (e.g., every few days). The fermentation process can take several weeks to several months. Do not be discouraged by unusual or strong smells (like skunky or nail polish remover) during this period. Continue until the mixture tastes sharply acidic, similar to store-bought vinegar.

Once the desired acidity is reached, carefully pour three-quarters of the finished vinegar into a clean, airtight bottle, minimizing empty space. This bottled vinegar can continue to age and improve in flavor.
You can use the remaining mother and pellicle in the original container to start a new batch by adding more wine.
